desc: Jaipur is one of Indias flagship heritage cities, known for forts, palace architecture, and dense artisan markets within a highly connected Golden Triangle route. The most comfortable season is roughly November-February, while pre-monsoon months are much hotter and monsoon weeks can affect day touring.
hls: Amber Fort and hilltop heritage complexes
City Palace and Pink City architecture
Strong crafts, textiles, and gemstone markets

todo.name: Amber Fort complex
todo.desc: Plan an early visit to avoid peak heat and crowds at the main fort site.

todo.name: City Palace + Jantar Mantar
todo.desc: Pair central royal architecture with the UNESCO observatory.

todo.name: Hawa Mahal area walk
todo.desc: Explore the Pink City facades and nearby bazaars on foot.

todo.name: Johari/Bapu bazaar shopping
todo.desc: Browse textiles, jewelry, and crafts with fixed-time bargain discipline.

todo.name: Nahargarh sunset viewpoint
todo.desc: Use elevated fort viewpoints for late-day city panoramas.

todo.name: Rajasthani dinner evening
todo.desc: Book a reputable regional restaurant or cultural dinner venue.

wifi.note: Good in mid-range and premium hotels; patchier reliability in budget properties.

prac.fltNote: No direct Gatwick-Jaipur service; UK itineraries usually connect via Delhi/Middle East hubs with domestic leg. Returns vary widely by season and connection choice.
prac.visa: UK and US travelers generally require a valid Indian e-visa or visa before arrival.
alerts: Heat can be extreme from April-June; schedule outdoor sightseeing early/late.
Traffic and travel times can be longer than expected; build buffers.
Monsoon showers can disrupt some day plans in July-September.

sweet: November-February is usually best for comfort and full-day sightseeing.
